newsSince: Incremental product/visibility news: CEO Matt Moschner gave the Automate 2026 opening keynote (June 22) on AI/robotics in automation, and Cognex is promoting its OneVision cloud platform plus In-Sight 3900/6900 edge-AI vision. No new financial guidance; Q2 2026 guide ($280M-$300M) was already set. Mildly positive narrative reinforcement, not a fundamental shift.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent industrial-AI vision node. OneVision/edge-AI launches and the Automate keynote modestly reinforce the AI-vision endpoint thesis, but the +75% YTD means it is increasingly discovered, not latent. Small positive drift, no rank move.
verdicts.bull: AI-vision platform (OneVision, In-Sight edge AI) plus Q1 +24% rev growth signals automation-capex recovery.
verdicts.neutral: Quality machine-vision franchise but cyclical and partly re-rated.
verdicts.bear: +75% YTD erodes the latent-discovery edge; factory-automation recovery still macro-dependent.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Factory automation capex recovery + AI-vision adoption
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical automation demand; crowd already returning

verdicts.rankRationale: On the humanoid-stack lens the link stays indirect: Cognex is factory/industrial machine vision, not a humanoid component. Automate keynote and OneVision are industrial-automation stories, not humanoid wins. Page thesis already discounts the rerating. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Machine-vision IP could feed robot perception over time; AI-vision platform launched.
verdicts.neutral: Vision matters to robotics but Cognex's exposure is industrial, not humanoid.
verdicts.bear: Indirect humanoid link, +75% YTD rerating; no humanoid design wins disclosed.
